The P is silent, as in pshrimp or pseudonym.

the only substack that is about weird medieval guys :)
Writings about writing (but also reading and other such things).
11 interesting stories handpicked by a curious human's algorithm.

Established in 2011, Five things on Friday is a weekly/occasional newsletter written by me, James Whatley.

It tries to be delivered to your inbox on Fridays but it often fails. Sometimes it arrives late. Sometimes it doesn’t arrive at all. But hey, it’s the thought that counts.

By day, I am an integrated strategist/planner working in advertising and communications. Often at the intersection of digital, entertainment, and brand.

Outside of working hours, I am a proud Dad, ardent gamer, and a regular roaster of potatoes.

As an interested human interested in interesting things, Five things on Friday is my way of a) collating the most interesting things I find on the internet and b) thinking out loud while reflecting on those things.

Along with work, life, culture and basically anything I feel like writing about.

If you're not sure then can find previous editions right here but lots of people tell me they like it so you might too.

So that’s my pitch.

Five things on Friday. It doesn’t always come on Friday but you always get more than five things.

Want in?
A short collection of five trivia items on a random topic, that can be read in less than a minute.

Explaining the weird new future, one newsletter at a time. Subscribe for a twice weekly delivery of internet culture, mega-platform grotesquerie, crypto conspiracies, deep forum lore, fringe politics, and other artifacts of what's to come.

Obsessively interested in everything.

A newsletter about taking control of our attention and finding a more human side of the internet. Featured in Wired and Rolling Stone.
I write essays about relationships, thought, and agency. I hang with our kids. We live on a pine covered island in the Baltic Sea. That's about it.

A newsletter for people thinking deeply about their work.

The art of noticing what matters. Finding and sharing the weird and wonderful patterns of the world

Chris Ferdinandi is a web developer with ADHD, helping people build the web better.
Recent content in Links on Harry Cresswell
Recent content in Newsletter on Harry Cresswell

"While I'm deeply sympathetic, the author should be discussing their issues with a therapist rather than spreading this on the internet."
I finally rage-quit and started an IT consultancy, Hermit Tech. If you're an engineer whose company is doing crazy things and there might still be room for an intervention (i.e, there must be at least one sane person in leadership), you can book time directly on my calendar and we can figure out a game plan! I've worked with some readers before and everyone had a good time. We've developed bold, innovative technology, such as not lying, the ability to explain things to laymen, and actually knowing how to ship on time. If you live in Melbourne, this final link straight-up arranges an in-person meeting at a nice coffee place. I also talk to most readers who want to chat, but you'll have to email me. I read all mail without exception, and love getting it. I may not respond, but I'll always read! You can reach me at: ludicity.hackernews@gmail.com. My podcast, "Does A Frog Have Scorpion Nature", where I talk to people I like, and sometimes find blog post inspiration. You can also catch me on Mastodon. Liberapay | Patreon to support me writing taking time off to write amidst the difficulty of launching a business.
High quality, curated design, dev and tech links. Twice a week.
A weekly newsletter about web development, tooling, and the latest trends in the web platform.
A short and useful newsletter for people who look after websites.
